title: Ownership, Borrowing, Aliasing, and API Contracts
description: A media index passes buffers through parsers, caches, and callbacks where no component knows who must release or may mutate them. Learners cumulatively produce ownership-annotated buffer API through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Classify each pointer as unique owner, shared owner, mutable borrow, immutable borrow, weak observation, or non-owning handle.
Make ownership transfer conditional on observable success and leave failure states unambiguous.
Bound every borrow by owner lifetime, mutation rules, callback duration, and asynchronous retention.
